text:
COMPREHENSIVE GENOMIC PROFILING REPORT
Solid Tumor Next-Generation Sequencing Panel (Hybrid Capture, Tumor-Only)
500-Gene Solid Tumor Panel

========================================================================
PATIENT & SPECIMEN INFORMATION
========================================================================
Patient Name: Margaret Yuen
Date of Birth: [REDACTED]
Sex: Female
Ordering Physician: [Attending Oncologist], MD
Department: Thoracic Oncology
Indication: Non-small cell lung carcinoma, adenocarcinoma, newly diagnosed Stage IIIB (T3N2M0) -- molecular profiling for treatment planning

Specimen ID: [LAB-XXXX-NGS]
Specimen Type: Formalin-fixed, paraffin-embedded (FFPE) core needle biopsy
Specimen Site: Right upper lobe primary lung mass (CT-guided core needle biopsy)
Date of Biopsy: [SEE PATHOLOGY REPORT]
Histologic Diagnosis (per concurrent pathology): Invasive pulmonary adenocarcinoma, acinar-predominant with focal lepidic component
Percent Tumor Nuclei: 55% (estimated)
Sequencing Platform: Hybrid capture-based 500-gene solid tumor panel (tumor-only, no matched normal)
Mean Coverage: 742x
Quality Control: PASS
Analysis Version: [Internal pipeline v3.6]

========================================================================
SUMMARY OF CLINICALLY RELEVANT FINDINGS
========================================================================

ACTIONABLE / CLINICALLY SIGNIFICANT ALTERATIONS:

  1. EGFR p.L858R (c.2573T>G), Exon 21
     Classification: SENSITIZING MUTATION -- TIER I (Level A Evidence)
     Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 42%
     Genomic coordinates: Chr7:55,259,515 T>G
     Interpretation: Classic EGFR exon 21 L858R activating point mutation. This is one of the two most common sensitizing EGFR mutations in non-small cell lung adenocarcinoma (along with exon 19 deletions), accounting for approximately 40-45% of EGFR-mutant NSCLC cases. This mutation confers established, high-level sensitivity to EGFR tyrosine kinase inhibitors (TKIs) of all three generations (Tarceva/gefi, afat, osi). Third-generation osi (Tagrisso) is preferred first-line therapy based on FLAURA trial data demonstrating superior PFS and OS over first-generation TKIs in EGFR-mutant NSCLC, with additional CNS penetration benefit. The VAF of 42% is consistent with a clonal, driver mutation.

CO-MUTATIONS AND ADDITIONAL VARIANTS:

  2. TP53 p.R248W (c.742C>T), Exon 7
     Classification: LIKELY PATHOGENIC -- TIER II (oncogenic, co-mutation)
     Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 38%
     Interpretation: TP53 R248W is a well-characterized gain-of-function hotspot mutation in the DNA-binding domain of p53. It is among the most frequently identified co-mutations in EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma, occurring in approximately 30-40% of cases. TP53 co-mutation with EGFR sensitizing mutations has been associated in some studies with modestly reduced duration of response to EGFR TKI therapy and may confer a higher risk of acquired resistance emergence; however, TP53 status alone does not alter the recommendation for first-line EGFR TKI therapy. This mutation is not targetable with currently approved agents.

  3. ARID1A p.E1814* (c.5440G>T), Exon 20
     Classification: VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS)
     Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 18%
     Interpretation: ARID1A encodes a subunit of the SWI/SNF chromatin remodeling complex. Truncating/loss-of-function alterations in ARID1A are recurrently observed in multiple tumor types including lung adenocarcinoma. At a VAF of 18%, this variant may represent a subclonal alteration. Its functional and clinical significance in this context is uncertain. No approved targeted therapy is available specifically for ARID1A-deficient tumors in lung cancer at this time. This finding may have future clinical relevance if the field advances. Serial monitoring of VAF may provide insight into clonal dynamics.

  4. KDR p.V297I (c.889G>A), Exon 7
     Classification: VARIANT OF UNCERTAIN SIGNIFICANCE (VUS)
     Variant Allele Frequency (VAF): 22%
     Interpretation: KDR (also known as VEGFR2) encodes vascular endothelial growth factor receptor 2. This missense variant (V297I) is in the extracellular ligand-binding domain. It has been observed in population databases and some tumor datasets, and its functional consequence is unclear. It is not classified as an activating or inactivating mutation based on current evidence. Clinical significance in the context of anti-angiogenic therapy (e.g., Avastin) is unknown.

========================================================================
WILD-TYPE / NOT DETECTED (SELECTED CLINICALLY RELEVANT GENES)
========================================================================
EGFR Exon 19 Deletion: NOT DETECTED
EGFR T790M (Exon 20): NOT DETECTED
EGFR Exon 20 Insertion: NOT DETECTED
EGFR C797S (Exon 20): NOT DETECTED
EGFR G719X (Exon 18): NOT DETECTED
EGFR S768I (Exon 20): NOT DETECTED
EGFR L861Q (Exon 21): NOT DETECTED
KRAS: WILD-TYPE (no G12/G13/Q61 hotspot mutations detected) -- NOTE: KRAS mutations are essentially mutually exclusive with EGFR sensitizing mutations; wild-type status is consistent with EGFR-driven adenocarcinoma biology
BRAF: WILD-TYPE (V600E not detected)
NRAS: WILD-TYPE
STK11 (LKB1): WILD-TYPE -- absence of STK11 co-mutation is a favorable feature in EGFR TKI-treated patients; STK11 loss has been associated with reduced immune responsiveness and worse outcomes with immunotherapy
KEAP1: WILD-TYPE -- absence of KEAP1 co-mutation; KEAP1 mutations are associated with resistance to EGFR TKI therapy in some EGFR-mutant adenocarcinomas
RB1: WILD-TYPE -- no loss-of-function alterations identified; RB1 intact status suggests no evidence of impending small cell transformation at this time
SMAD4: WILD-TYPE
NF1: WILD-TYPE
PIK3CA: WILD-TYPE
PTEN: WILD-TYPE
ERBB2 (HER2): WILD-TYPE; no exon 20 insertion detected; no amplification
MET: WILD-TYPE; no exon 14 skip mutation detected

========================================================================
FUSION / REARRANGEMENT ANALYSIS
========================================================================
ALK Rearrangement: NOT DETECTED (consistent with ALK IHC negative per pathology report)
ROS1 Rearrangement: NOT DETECTED (consistent with ROS1 FISH negative per pathology report)
RET Rearrangement: NOT DETECTED
NTRK1 Rearrangement: NOT DETECTED
NTRK2 Rearrangement: NOT DETECTED
NTRK3 Rearrangement: NOT DETECTED
MET Exon 14 Skip Mutation: NOT DETECTED

Note: EGFR and kinase fusion drivers are highly mutually exclusive in lung adenocarcinoma; the absence of ALK, ROS1, RET, and NTRK fusions is expected and consistent with an EGFR-driven tumor.

========================================================================
COPY NUMBER ALTERATIONS
========================================================================
MET: Copy Number 2 (diploid; NO amplification detected)
  -- Note: MET copy number within normal range at initial diagnosis; baseline established for future comparison
ERBB2 (HER2): Copy Number 2 (diploid; NO amplification detected)
CDK6: Low-level copy number gain (copy number approximately 3-4; borderline, below threshold for high-level amplification)
  -- Potential cell cycle regulatory implication; clinical significance uncertain in this context
CDK4: NOT AMPLIFIED
EGFR: Low-level gain (polysomy, expected in lung adenocarcinoma; not considered high-level amplification)
MYC: NOT AMPLIFIED
FGFR1: NOT AMPLIFIED
All other assessed genes within normal copy number range.

========================================================================
TUMOR MUTATIONAL BURDEN (TMB)
========================================================================
TMB Score: 6.2 mutations/megabase (mut/Mb)
Classification: LOW (threshold for TMB-High: >=10 mut/Mb per FDA-cleared assay standard)
Interpretation: Low TMB is consistent with the molecular profile of EGFR-mutant lung adenocarcinoma in a former light smoker (20 pack-years, quit 12 years prior). EGFR-mutant NSCLCs characteristically exhibit low TMB relative to KRAS-mutant or tobacco-heavy mutational profiles. Low TMB predicts limited likelihood of durable benefit from single-agent immune checkpoint inhibitor therapy; however, it does not affect the recommendation for EGFR TKI therapy.

========================================================================
MICROSATELLITE INSTABILITY (MSI) STATUS
========================================================================
MSI Status: MICROSATELLITE STABLE (MSS)
Interpretation: MSS status is consistent with lung adenocarcinoma in general and with the low TMB observed. MSI-High is a very uncommon finding in primary lung adenocarcinoma.

========================================================================
PD-L1 EXPRESSION (FROM CONCURRENT PATHOLOGY)
========================================================================
Note: PD-L1 expression was assessed by immunohistochemistry (Dako 22C3 assay) on the concurrent pathology specimen.
PD-L1 TPS: 15% (low positive)
Interpretation: While a PD-L1 TPS of 15% is formally positive, this score falls below the TPS >=50% threshold associated with Keytruda (Keytruda) monotherapy benefit. Importantly, in the context of EGFR-sensitizing mutation positivity, immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy or combination with chemotherapy is not recommended as first-line therapy due to lack of benefit and potential for increased toxicity (particularly immune-related pneumonitis in EGFR-mutant tumors treated with anti-PD-(L)1 agents concurrently with TKIs). PD-L1 status is informative but secondary to EGFR mutation status for treatment selection in this case.

========================================================================
GENOMIC INTERPRETATION AND CLINICAL CONTEXT
========================================================================
This comprehensive genomic profiling result from the primary right upper lobe adenocarcinoma identifies EGFR p.L858R as the primary oncogenic driver mutation (VAF 42%), with co-occurring TP53 p.R248W (VAF 38%) and subclonal variants of uncertain significance (ARID1A p.E1814*, KDR p.V297I). The genomic landscape is consistent with EGFR-driven pulmonary adenocarcinoma in a patient with a light-to-moderate smoking history.

Key prognostic and predictive features of this profile:
  - EGFR L858R is a Tier I actionable sensitizing mutation with high-level evidence for EGFR TKI benefit (FDA-approved: osi, Tarceva, Iressa, Gilotrif, Vizimpro, erlo + ramu).
  - Absence of co-occurring STK11 or KEAP1 mutations is a favorable feature; these co-mutations have been associated with reduced TKI efficacy and more aggressive disease biology in some analyses.
  - Absence of RB1 mutation at baseline is notable; RB1 loss is associated with small cell transformation risk, which can occur as a resistance mechanism to EGFR TKI therapy, particularly with prolonged TKI exposure.
  - TP53 co-mutation (R248W, gain-of-function hotspot) at a high VAF clonal level may be associated with modestly inferior TKI outcomes compared to TP53 wild-type EGFR-mutant NSCLC, but does not change initial treatment selection.
  - Low TMB and MSS status indicate low likelihood of benefit from immune checkpoint inhibitor monotherapy.
  - No baseline T790M, C797S, MET amplification, or ERBB2 amplification identified at initial diagnosis (these are common acquired resistance mechanisms to EGFR TKIs and will be important to monitor at time of progression).
  - Low-level CDK6 copy number gain is of uncertain significance and does not currently warrant targeted intervention.

Alteration summary table:
  Gene        | Alteration          | VAF  | Classification              | Therapy Implication
  ------------|---------------------|------|-----------------------------|---------------------
  EGFR        | p.L858R (Exon 21)   | 42%  | Sensitizing / Tier I        | EGFR TKI (osi preferred 1L)
  TP53        | p.R248W (Exon 7)    | 38%  | Likely Pathogenic (co-mut)  | No targeted therapy available
  ARID1A      | p.E1814* (Exon 20)  | 18%  | VUS                         | None currently
  KDR         | p.V297I (Exon 7)    | 22%  | VUS                         | None currently
  STK11       | Wild-type           | --   | Favorable                   | N/A
  KEAP1       | Wild-type           | --   | Favorable                   | N/A
  RB1         | Wild-type           | --   | Favorable (no SC risk)      | N/A
  MET         | CN 2 (no amp)       | --   | Negative                    | N/A at baseline
  ERBB2       | CN 2 (no amp)       | --   | Negative                    | N/A
  Fusions     | None detected       | --   | Negative                    | N/A

========================================================================
QUALITY ASSURANCE NOTES
========================================================================
- Tumor-only sequencing: germline variants cannot be excluded without matched normal sequencing. Variants in TP53, ARID1A, and KDR should be interpreted with this limitation in mind; however, at these VAFs and in this oncologic context, somatic origin is likely.
- Sensitivity for structural variants (fusions, large deletions) may be lower than for SNVs/indels on this hybrid-capture panel; RNA-based fusion analysis was not performed on this specimen. Given ALK and ROS1 negativity by IHC/FISH, this is not expected to affect clinical management.
- FFPE-related artifact was assessed and controlled for; artifact-associated false positives are not expected to be contributing to the reported variants.

========================================================================
SIGNATURE
========================================================================
Molecular Pathologist: [Molecular Pathologist Name], MD, PhD
Board Certification: AP/CP, Molecular Genetic Pathology
Report Reviewed By: [Senior Reviewer], MD
Report Status: FINAL

This report was generated for clinical use in the context of a newly diagnosed Stage IIIB EGFR L858R-mutant pulmonary adenocarcinoma. Results should be correlated with all clinical, radiographic, and pathologic findings. This report does not constitute a clinical management recommendation; treatment decisions remain the responsibility of the treating physician in consultation with the multidisciplinary team.
